verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the May 28 Oppenheimer upgrade ($800 PT) and Q1 backlog strength were already reflected in yesterday's down move for crowding. Today's -1.5% drift off the early-June high neither adds evidence nor meaningfully restores asymmetry, so hold at rank 96.
verdicts.bull: Near-$50B backlog, raised FY26 guidance ($34.7-35.2B revenue) and a $1B buyback give durable grid/AI-power buildout exposure; a continued pullback from ~$715 toward the low $600s would rebuild entry asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: A $104B large-cap up 55% YTD with sell-side now fully on board (Oppenheimer Outperform, $800 PT) is a well-told story; it earns its Monitor/benchmark bucket but offers little hidden alpha versus less-rerated passive/power bottleneck names.
verdicts.bear: Crowding plus a 30-35x EBITDA-multiple-anchored bull case leaves the stock exposed to any AI-capex digestion or utility-spend deceleration headline; labor-intensive E&C margins cap upside surprise versus component bottlenecks.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings and backlog/bookings update, plus any large transmission or data-center-power contract awards.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression on AI-infrastructure sentiment reversal given full sell-side endorsement and 55% YTD rerating.
